name: Saenz v. Roe
summary: Citizenship clause of Fourteenth Amendment protects right of newly arrived citizens to same privileges and immunities as long-term residents; durational residency requirements for welfare benefits unconstitutional.
holding: Citizenship clause protects right to travel, including right to be treated equally in new state of residence; California cannot limit welfare benefits for new residents to levels of prior state for first year.
constitutional_basis: Fourteenth Amendment Citizenship Clause, Privileges and Immunities
relevance_to_audit: Reaffirms constitutional protection for interstate travel and equal treatment; relevant to residency requirements, movement restrictions, and discriminatory treatment of newcomers.
